agreement.name: LIBERAL STUDIES, Arts and Humanities Emphasis, B.A.

notes.content: Liberal Studies is designed to allow students transferring as juniors and enrolled full-time to complete all degree requirements within two years. This interdisciplinary major brings together the social sciences, humanities, and arts, with the option to pursue an emphasis area and tailor a flexible program of study. Students develop strong skills in critical thinking, problem-solving, and communication, preparing them for a wide range of career paths and graduate study opportunities.
notes.contentHtml: Liberal Studies is designed to allow students transferring as juniors and enrolled full-time to complete all degree requirements within two years. This interdisciplinary major brings together the social sciences, humanities, and arts, with the option to pursue an emphasis area and tailor a flexible program of study. Students develop strong skills in critical thinking, problem-solving, and communication, preparing them for a wide range of career paths and graduate study opportunities.

notes.content: The General Transfer Requirements are as follows: Units and GPA -Complete 60 semester (90 quarter) units of UC-transferable college credit -Earn a minimum grade point average of 2.40 (nonresident: 2.80) in UC-transferable courses Take no more than 14 semester (21 quarter) units as Pass/Not Pass Course Requirements/Subject Requirements -Complete the following seven-course pattern, earning a grade of C- or better in each course: -Two transferable English composition courses -One transferable mathematical concepts and quantitative reasoning course that has a published prerequisite of intermediate algebra -Four transferable courses chosen from at least two of the following areas: Arts and Humanities, Behavioral and Social Sciences, Biological and Physical Sciences **Other than the UC Merced requirements, applicants do not have to fulfill any additional requirements. The TAG Requirements below differ from Transfer Selection** NEW TAG Approved: Transfer Admissions Guarantee (TAG): Liberal Studies will participate in the Transfer Admissions Guarantee program. For students entering through TAG, the following requirements apply: -GPA: Minimum transferable unit GPA of 2.8, or higher -Course Requirements: One UC transferable course in Social Sciences and one UC transferable course in Humanities and Arts Transfer Admissions Guarantee (TAG): UC Merced is one of the six UC's that offers Transfer Admissions Guarantee: Please visit the TAG website for more information: https://admissions.ucmerced.edu/transfer/tag
notes.contentHtml:
The General Transfer Requirements are as follows:


Units and GPA

-Complete 60 semester (90 quarter) units of UC-transferable college credit

-Earn a minimum grade point average of 2.40 (nonresident: 2.80) in UC-transferable courses

Take no more than 14 semester (21 quarter) units as Pass/Not Pass


Course Requirements/Subject Requirements

-Complete the following seven-course pattern, earning a grade of C- or better in each course:

-Two transferable English composition courses

-One transferable mathematical concepts and quantitative reasoning course that has a published prerequisite of intermediate algebra

-Four transferable courses chosen from at least two of the following areas: Arts and Humanities, Behavioral and Social Sciences, Biological and Physical Sciences


**Other than the UC Merced requirements, applicants do not have to fulfill any additional requirements. The TAG Requirements below differ from Transfer Selection**

NEW TAG Approved: Transfer Admissions Guarantee (TAG): Liberal Studies will participate in the Transfer Admissions Guarantee program.

For students entering through TAG, the following requirements apply:

-GPA: Minimum transferable unit GPA of 2.8, or higher

-Course Requirements: One UC transferable course in Social Sciences and one UC transferable course in Humanities and Arts


Transfer Admissions Guarantee (TAG): UC Merced is one of the six UC's that offers Transfer Admissions Guarantee: Please visit the TAG website for more information: https://admissions.ucmerced.edu/transfer/tag

notes.content: Recommended, but not required for Admission

While major courses are not required for admission, it is highly recommended that students complete as many recommended major preparatory courses as possible before transferring to support timely progress to degree. Any major courses not completed prior to enrollment at UC Merced must be taken after transfer to stay on track for graduation. To identify which courses may transfer, students should use the Department or Prefix search in ASSIST and look up articulated courses under the relevant subject areas (for example, WRI, HIST, PHIL, etc.). Please note: Liberal Studies and each emphasis track have different requirements; please review the information for each area to ensure completion of the appropriate preparatory coursework. This major is currently under development, and finalized information will be published in the 2026–2027 catalog. Cornerstone Requirement -LBST 001 (Coming Soon) Social Sciences – Complete 8 units in the following subjects: ANTH; COGS; CRS; ECON; GEOG; HS; MGMT; PH; POLI; PSY Approved courses: -ANTH 001, 003, 005, 010, 033 -COGS 001, 005, 013 -CRS 010 -ECON 001, 001H, 005, 006A, 006B, 007, 010, 050 -GEOG 002, 010 -HS 001, 002, 003, 004, 010 -MGMT (no applicable lower-division courses) -PH 001, 005, 010 -POLI 001, 002, 003, 005, 006, 009, 010 -PSY 001, 010, 015 Social Sciences or Humanties and Arts Requirment: Complete four additional lower division units in either Social Sciences or Humanities and Arts from any of the following subjects*: ANTH; CCST; CHN; COGS; CRES; CRS; ECON; EH; ENG; FRE; GASP; GEOG; HIST; HS; JPN; MGMT; PH; PHIL; POLI; PSY; SPAN; WRI *For students who select Environmental Humanities as a subject area, this requirement is waived. For students who select other subject areas, up to four units of lower division STEM coursework can substitute, with a petition process Approved courses: -CCST 014, 060 -CHN 001, 002, 003, 004 -CRES 001, 011, 012, 020, 022, 027, 030, 039, 040, 043, 051, 052, 059A, 070, 072B, 076A, 076B -EH 001, 019, 059, 060, 067 -ENG 001, 010, 011, 012, 018, 019, 020, 022, 030, 031, 032, 033, 034, 049, 050, 051, 052, 054, 055, 056, 057, 058, 059, 060, 061, 062, 063, 064, 065, 066, 067, 071, 089 -FRE 001, 002, 003, 004 -GASP 001, 002, 003, 004, 006, 007, 010, 011, 012A, 013A, 014, 015A, 020, 026A, 030A, 030B, 030C, 030D, 030E, 031A, 031B, 031C, 031D, 034A, 034B, 034C, 036A, 036B, 041A, 055A, 055B, 055C, 055D, 056, 057, 059, 059A, 060, 060A, 064A, 064B, 065A, 066A, 069A, 070A, 070B, 070C, 072A, 072B, 075A, 076B, 079A, 079B, 080A, 080B, 089A -HIST 005, 008, 010, 011, 012, 016, 017, 020, 021, 022, 027, 030, 030B, 035, 039, 040, 041, 042, 043, 051, 052, 055, 055A, 055D, 060, 070, 071 -JPN 001, 002, 003, 004 -PHIL 001, 002, 003, 004, 005, 006, 008, 009 -SPAN 001, 002, 003, 004, 010, 011, 014, 050, 060 -WRI 001, 001A, 010, 010H, 011, 022, 025, 030 Humanities and Arts Specializations: Upper-division requirements for the Liberal Studies, Arts and Humanities Emphasis, B.A. are completed at UC Merced and vary by specialization. Applicants should complete the lower-division preparatory coursework that aligns with their intended specialization. Students must select the same specialization for both their lower-division and upper-division requirements. The following outlines the lower-division requirements for each specialization.. American Studies - HIST 016 and HIST 017 Chicanx Studies -CCST 060 or SPAN 060 or ENG 032 -Four additional lower-division units from: CCST, CHN, CRES, EH, ENG, FRE, GASP, HIST, JPN, PHIL, SPAN, or WRI Critical Race and Ethnic Studies -CRES 001 -Four additional lower-division units from: CCST, CHN, CRES, EH, ENG, FRE, GASP, HIST, JPN, PHIL, SPAN, or WRI Environmental Humanities -EH 001 -One additional lower-division course from: ENG 019, ENG 058, ENG 059, ENG 060, ENG 067 -One additional lower-division course from: COGS 013, ENGR 096, ESS 002, ESS 010, ESS 034, BIO 034, ESS 043, ESS 050, ENCE 010, ENVE 020, ENVE 030, PHYS 001 Global Arts Studies -Complete two lower-division GASP courses History -Complete two lower-division HIST courses Literatures in English -One lower-division seminar ENG course (ENG 050–ENG 090) -One additional lower-division ENG course (ENG 010–ENG 090) Philosophy -One of the following: PHIL 001, PHIL 002, PHIL 003, PHIL 004, PHIL 005, PHIL 008, or PHIL 009 -Four additional lower-division units from: CCST, CHN, CRES, EH, ENG, FRE, GASP, HIST, JPN, PHIL, SPAN, or WRI Spanish -Eight lower-division units from: CCST, CHN, CRES, EH, ENG, FRE, GASP, HIST, JPN, PHIL, SPAN, or WRI* *For students who complete SPAN 050 as an upper-division specialization requirement, only four additional lower-division units are required. Writing Studies -One of the following: WRI 025, WRI 030, or WRI 090 -Four additional lower-division units from: CCST, CHN, CRES, EH, ENG, FRE, GASP, HIST, JPN, PHIL, SPAN, or WRI AP Exam Score and Course Exemptions -An AP Statistics score of 4 exempts BIO 018 or PSY 010 -An AP Statistics score of 5 exempts ECON 010 or SOC 010 -An AP Psychology score of 4 or 5 exempts PSY 001 -AP History: US History: score of 4 or 5 exempts HIST 016 and HIST 017 -AP History: World History: Modern: score of 4 or 5 exempts HIST 010 or HIST 011 Visit the UC Merced Advance Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) page to view additional AP credit scores and credit policies in the link below: Advance Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examinations For questions about admissions, please email: transfer@ucmerced.edu .
notes.contentHtml:
While major courses are not required for admission, it is highly recommended that students complete as many recommended major preparatory courses as possible before transferring to support timely progress to degree. Any major courses not completed prior to enrollment at UC Merced must be taken after transfer to stay on track for graduation. To identify which courses may transfer, students should use the Department or Prefix search in ASSIST and look up articulated courses under the relevant subject areas (for example, WRI, HIST, PHIL, etc.).


Please note: Liberal Studies and each emphasis track have different requirements; please review the information for each area to ensure completion of the appropriate preparatory coursework. This major is currently under development, and finalized information will be published in the 2026–2027 catalog.


Cornerstone Requirement

-LBST 001 (Coming Soon)

Social Sciences – Complete 8 units in the following subjects: ANTH; COGS; CRS; ECON; GEOG; HS; MGMT; PH; POLI; PSY

Approved courses:

-ANTH 001, 003, 005, 010, 033

-COGS 001, 005, 013

-CRS 010

-ECON 001, 001H, 005, 006A, 006B, 007, 010, 050

-GEOG 002, 010

-HS 001, 002, 003, 004, 010

-MGMT (no applicable lower-division courses)

-PH 001, 005, 010

-POLI 001, 002, 003, 005, 006, 009, 010

-PSY 001, 010, 015


Social Sciences or Humanties and Arts Requirment: Complete four additional lower division units in either Social Sciences or Humanities and Arts from any of the following subjects*: ANTH; CCST; CHN; COGS; CRES; CRS; ECON; EH; ENG; FRE; GASP; GEOG; HIST; HS; JPN; MGMT; PH; PHIL; POLI; PSY; SPAN; WRI

*For students who select Environmental Humanities as a subject area, this requirement is waived. For students who select other subject areas, up to four units of lower division STEM coursework can substitute, with a petition process

Approved courses:

-CCST 014, 060

-CHN 001, 002, 003, 004

-CRES 001, 011, 012, 020, 022, 027, 030, 039, 040, 043, 051, 052, 059A, 070, 072B, 076A, 076B

-EH 001, 019, 059, 060, 067

-ENG 001, 010, 011, 012, 018, 019, 020, 022, 030, 031, 032, 033, 034, 049, 050, 051, 052, 054, 055, 056, 057, 058, 059, 060, 061, 062, 063, 064, 065, 066, 067, 071, 089

-FRE 001, 002, 003, 004

-GASP 001, 002, 003, 004, 006, 007, 010, 011, 012A, 013A, 014, 015A, 020, 026A, 030A, 030B, 030C, 030D, 030E, 031A, 031B, 031C, 031D, 034A, 034B, 034C, 036A, 036B, 041A, 055A, 055B, 055C, 055D, 056, 057, 059, 059A, 060, 060A, 064A, 064B, 065A, 066A, 069A, 070A, 070B, 070C, 072A, 072B, 075A, 076B, 079A, 079B, 080A, 080B, 089A

-HIST 005, 008, 010, 011, 012, 016, 017, 020, 021, 022, 027, 030, 030B, 035, 039, 040, 041, 042, 043, 051, 052, 055, 055A, 055D, 060, 070, 071

-JPN 001, 002, 003, 004

-PHIL 001, 002, 003, 004, 005, 006, 008, 009

-SPAN 001, 002, 003, 004, 010, 011, 014, 050, 060

-WRI 001, 001A, 010, 010H, 011, 022, 025, 030


Humanities and Arts Specializations:

Upper-division requirements for the Liberal Studies, Arts and Humanities Emphasis, B.A. are completed at UC Merced and vary by specialization. Applicants should complete the lower-division preparatory coursework that aligns with their intended specialization. Students must select the same specialization for both their lower-division and upper-division requirements. The following outlines the lower-division requirements for each specialization..

American Studies

-HIST 016 and HIST 017

Chicanx Studies

-CCST 060 or SPAN 060 or ENG 032

-Four additional lower-division units from: CCST, CHN, CRES, EH, ENG, FRE, GASP, HIST, JPN, PHIL, SPAN, or WRI

Critical Race and Ethnic Studies

-CRES 001

-Four additional lower-division units from: CCST, CHN, CRES, EH, ENG, FRE, GASP, HIST, JPN, PHIL, SPAN, or WRI

Environmental Humanities

-EH 001

-One additional lower-division course from: ENG 019, ENG 058, ENG 059, ENG 060, ENG 067

-One additional lower-division course from: COGS 013, ENGR 096, ESS 002, ESS 010, ESS 034, BIO 034, ESS 043, ESS 050, ENCE 010, ENVE 020, ENVE 030, PHYS 001

Global Arts Studies

-Complete two lower-division GASP courses

History

-Complete two lower-division HIST courses

Literatures in English

-One lower-division seminar ENG course (ENG 050–ENG 090)

-One additional lower-division ENG course (ENG 010–ENG 090)

Philosophy

-One of the following: PHIL 001, PHIL 002, PHIL 003, PHIL 004, PHIL 005, PHIL 008, or PHIL 009

-Four additional lower-division units from: CCST, CHN, CRES, EH, ENG, FRE, GASP, HIST, JPN, PHIL, SPAN, or WRI

Spanish

-Eight lower-division units from: CCST, CHN, CRES, EH, ENG, FRE, GASP, HIST, JPN, PHIL, SPAN, or WRI*

*For students who complete SPAN 050 as an upper-division specialization requirement, only four additional lower-division units are required.

Writing Studies

-One of the following: WRI 025, WRI 030, or WRI 090

-Four additional lower-division units from: CCST, CHN, CRES, EH, ENG, FRE, GASP, HIST, JPN, PHIL, SPAN, or WRI


AP Exam Score and Course Exemptions

-An AP Statistics score of 4 exempts BIO 018 or PSY 010

-An AP Statistics score of 5 exempts ECON 010 or SOC 010

-An AP Psychology score of 4 or 5 exempts PSY 001

-AP History: US History: score of 4 or 5 exempts HIST 016 and HIST 017

-AP History: World History: Modern: score of 4 or 5 exempts HIST 010 or HIST 011

Visit the UC Merced Advance Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) page to view additional AP credit scores and credit policies in the link below:

Advance Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examinations



For questions about admissions, please email: transfer@ucmerced.edu.
